This learning path offers actionable guidance, from identifying high-impact use cases and understanding cost drivers, to forecasting ROI, adopting best practices, designing scalable and effective architectures, and optimizing ongoing investments.

Title: Explore SQL Server backups
Description: In this learning path, you'll embark on an insightful exploration of Microsoft’s Responsible AI framework, learning how to apply ethical principles to the development and use of AI technologies. You start by learning the frameworks that shape Microsoft’s approach to responsible AI, focusing on the commitment to the six responsible AI principles. As you progress, you discover practical ways to integrate these principles into educational environments, ensuring that AI tools not only enhance learning experiences but also uphold values of fairness and privacy. The learning path concludes with hands-on exercises, translating theoretical knowledge into real-world practice. This learning path equips you with the necessary skills to implement responsible AI systems responsibly in any learning environment.

Title: Import and configure resources
Description: Learn to design and implement database objects, programmability objects, advanced T-SQL code, and AI-assisted tools for SQL Server, Azure SQL, and Microsoft Fabric.

Additionally, gain valuable insights on how to initiate the implementation of GitHub Copilot within your organization, enabling you to kickstart your own transformative journey.

Title: Summary and resources
Description: Learn how to use Transact-SQL (T-SQL) to interact with relational databases by querying and modifying data. This course covers essential operations such as inserting, updating, deleting, and merging data across tables. You'll also explore how to generate automatic values and apply changes efficiently using SQL Server or Azure SQL environments.

Description: At Microsoft, we understand the power of technology, and that’s why we continue to innovate, create, and design in a way that gives everyone the ability to achieve more. Trainings like this learning path, which covers accessibility fundamentals, are an important and necessary part of Microsoft’s accessibility strategy to support customers of all abilities. Don’t forget to share your awards on social media after completing each module!

Title: Module assessment
Description: Learn how to perform online and offline SQL Server migrations to Azure SQL. (DP-3001)

Title: Introduction

This learning path is crafted for IT and security professionals tasked with maintaining data protection and compliance in AI environments. Discover how to prevent data exfiltration and apply governance policies that ensure consistent, secure AI deployments. Learn to combine technical controls with strategic oversight to create resilient, policy-driven AI architectures that align with organizational and regulatory standards.

Title: Configure auditing for SQL Managed Instance
Description: The GitHub Foundations Learning Path Part 2 of 2 is a concise and beginner-friendly journey designed to introduce you to the fundamental concepts and products of GitHub.

Title: Summary and resources
Description: Implement a defense-in-depth security strategy for Azure Key Vault. Configure hardened vault settings, control access through RBAC and just-in-time privilege, manage keys, secrets, and certificates, and detect threats using Microsoft Defender for Key Vault and Defender CSPM.

Title: Explore the Windows Server File Server high-availability options
Description: PWABuilder is an open-source tool developed by Microsoft that streamlines the process of deploying Progressive Web Apps (PWAs). It simplifies PWA development by automating the creation of essential components, providing a user-friendly interface for customization, and packaging PWAs for various platforms and app stores. Learn how to generate the app manifest, configure the service worker, and customize your PWA's settings and appearance to transform your project into a polished final product. Discover how to use PWABuilder to test PWAs on various devices and platforms, reserve app names, and successfully package a PWA for Microsoft Store submission.

Title: Design a messaging solution
Description: In this learning path, learn the basics of GitHub administration and discover the products that GitHub offers. This is the second learning path in a two part series.

Title: Explore the available data migration technologies
Description: Learn how to design, deploy, and manage agentic AI systems within the software development lifecycle by integrating, orchestrating, and governing autonomous agents using GitHub to ensure safe, reliable, and scalable delivery.

Title: Choose the right Azure SQL Database feature
Description: In this learning path, retail business leaders find all the knowledge and resources to adopt AI in their organizations. It discusses available resources, strategy, responsible practices, and retail use cases.

Title: Apply sensitivity labels in Power BI

With .NET you can use popular microservice technology like Docker, Kubernetes, Dapr, Azure Container Registry, and more for .NET and ASP.NET Core applications and services.

Learn how to design, configure, and manage DLP policies using Microsoft Purview. This learning path aligns with exam SC-401: Microsoft Information Security Administrator.

Explore prompt engineering, navigate the limitations of content created by generative AI models, and learn to utilize generative AI models into your trainings critically and responsibly.

This is the second learning path out of two learning paths of GHAS.

You’ll explore how to determine the right time to modernize from Windows Server 2016 or Windows Server 2019, assess the role of Azure Local in your infrastructure strategy, and understand the key capabilities and positioning of Windows Server 2025. By the end of this learning path, you’ll be able to evaluate modernization approaches and identify how Windows Server 2025 can help meet evolving security, operational, and business requirements.

Take a tour of the transformative power of the cloud to drive business success, growth and cost savings with Microsoft Azure as your trusted partner. With practical insights and actual case studies, three streamlined modules cover the basics of cloud for business, the many business benefits of Azure, and best practices for transitioning your business to the cloud. This learning path, created especially for non-technical business leaders, requires no technical background.

Title: Summary
Description: This learning path introduces Microsoft productivity solutions designed to support everyday work across personal and business scenarios. Learners explore how Microsoft Office Home 2024 and Office Home & Business 2024 provide core desktop applications through a one-time purchase model, including when each option is the right fit based on usage needs, features, and other considerations. The learning path also introduces Microsoft 365 Copilot workflows, demonstrating how AI can be used alongside familiar tools to streamline tasks such as summarizing content, analyzing data, and improving communication across apps. By completing this learning path, learners will be able to evaluate different productivity solutions, understand how they support common workflows, and apply AI-powered capabilities to work more effectively in everyday tasks.
